page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.large is an entry-level virtual server belonging to the ecs.e family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 shared vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates entry-level performance characteristics. Stress-ng single-core CPU performance is measured at 1301.89, while multi-core performance reaches 1724.23, placing it in the lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth tests show 96.11 GB/sec for small cached blocks, but this drops significantly to 2.0 GB/sec for large uncached read operations and 2.04 GB/sec for large uncached write operations. Passmark CPU and memory scores are 1747.41 and 554.36 respectively, reflecting its shared resource architecture.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a clear tradeoff, as compute resources are shared with other tenants, leading to variable performance under sustained loads. However, this architecture offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that do not require continuous high-performance compute. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as basic static web serving, development environments, testing, and low-traffic background processes.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.large is an entry-level, budget-friendly virtual server within the ecs.e family. Powered by the KVM hypervisor, it features 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 4.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate modest performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 1724.23 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 2.0 GB/sec. Due to its shared CPU allocation, the server exhibits performance tradeoffs under heavy workloads but provides high qualitative cost efficiency for resource-light applications. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and lightweight background tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.large is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for lightweight development and basic web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m2.large is a budget virtual server with 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and KVM virtualization for basic workloads.
tagline: An entry-level shared virtual server designed for cost-effective development, testing, and lightweight background processing workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Entry-level performance profile suited for budget-conscious deployments
Appropriate for basic web serving, testing, and development environments
